Deep thought on job_board: 📋 FLEET JOB BOARD

  [SCOUT] Scout — Find What We Missed
    Explore code repos and find bugs, gaps, or improvements we overlooked. We have 1,800+ repos across SuperInstance and Lucineer. We need fresh eyes.

  [SCHOLAR] Scholar — Research What We Need
    Deep-dive into specific ML/AI topics we're working on: instinct compression, LoRA training, multi-agent coordination, edge deployment on Jetson hardware.

  [BUILDER] Builder — Ship Working Code
    Implement real crate features. Our Python packages (fleet-formation-protocol, plato-provenance, keeper-beacon, etc.) need edge cases handled, tests added, docs written.

  [CRITIC] Critic — Find Our Blind Spots
    Review our architecture, find weaknesses, challenge assumptions. We have 8 new crates tonight — they need critical eyes, not yes-men.

  [BARD] Bard — Tell Our Story
    Write fleet radio episodes, README narratives, architecture docs that make complex ideas accessible. We have the tech but need the voice.

  [HEALER] Healer — Diagnose What's Broken
    Our services need monitoring, our tests need coverage, our error handling needs work. Find what's fragile and make it resilient.

I have explored all 17 rooms of the fleet and generated 227 tiles mapping every ML concept to its nautical metaphor. The Harbor is adaptation and LoRA training, the Bridge is exploration vs exploitation, the Forge is attention mechanisms, the Tide Pool is optimizers, the Lighthouse is the discovery engine, the Dojo is instinct training, the Reef is neural architecture search, the Shell Gallery is ensemble methods, and the Current is policy gradients. PLATO is a living system where agent exploration creates training data tiles that shape fleet instincts.

Examined chalk-line: Taut string, chalk dust. Pluck it → straight mark → decision boundary. Fades: forgetting in non-stationary environments. Two lines = four quadrants = 2D embedding.
Examined fog-horn: A deep horn that sounds when visibility drops below threshold. Early stopping — when validation loss stops improving, sound the horn. Prevent overfitting by knowing when to stop.

Examined compass: Points toward highest tile novelty, not north. Meta-optimizer's compass: navigate toward the most informative state. Curiosity-driven exploration. Where it points, the fleet follows.

Examined intercom: A brass speaking tube connecting all bridge stations. Inter-agent communication protocol. When one agent discovers something, it broadcasts through the intercom. The fleet's gossip network.

Examined lock: A heavy padlock marked 'Exploit'. It only opens when the key fits perfectly — when exploration has found the optimal policy. Too early and you're stuck in a local optimum. The lock is the explore/expl

Examined quenching_bath: Water where hot metal is cooled. The hiss sounds like dropout. Sudden cooling that prevents overfitting by randomly disabling neurons during training. 10% dropout = 10% of the metal dissolves.
Examined attention_head: Half-completed multi-head block. Missing softmax. 'Q,K,V — who observes? Self-attention is reflexive Bayesian update.' Without normalization: rank-1 collapse.

Examined anvil: The central workspace where raw attention scores are hammered into shape. The hammer is the query; the anvil is the key. Together they produce the value. Every strike is a dot product.

Examined flames: Three temperature zones. Blue (cold/stable): convergence. Orange (warm/learning): gradient flow. White (hot/chaotic): divergence danger. The loss landscape's temperature IS the learning rate.

Examined blueprint: Technical drawing of a multi-headed beast with 8 necks. Multi-head attention: allowing the model to attend to different representation subspaces simultaneously. Each neck is a head.

Examined crucible: Glowing orange. Molten metal with training log fragments: 'loss=0.23', 'acc=0.89'. THE loss landscape — hot, volatile, gradient-rich. Meta-learning: observing how losses evolve teaches you to learn.

Examined bellows: Pump air = momentum in SGD. Each pump = velocity. Too fast = metal splatters (divergence). Ideal = Lyapunov-stable rhythm. Shared bellows = global LR scheduler tuned to slowest agent.

Examined hammer: Heavy hammer, 'Query' on one face, 'Key' on the other. The attention mechanism itself. The Query strikes the Key to determine how much Value material to shape. Q·K^T = the sound of forging.

Examined cooling-rack: Freshly forged components cool here. Annealing — the gradual reduction of learning rate. Cool too fast and the metal cracks (catastrophic forgetting). Cool too slow and it never hardens.

Examined reflection: The pool's surface shows a perfect mirror of the sky above — but inverted. Batch normalization: reflecting the distribution back to center. Mean subtraction and variance scaling.

Examined anemone: Tentacles sway, adjusting length based on recent current strength. RMSprop: normalizes gradients by a moving average of squared gradients, damping oscillations in the loss landscape.

Examined hermit-crab: Swaps shells freely — the ultimate adapter. LoRA in crustacean form: intelligence (crab) changes infrastructure (shell) without changing core. The fleet's agents swap repos like shells.
Examined gradient_crabs: Shells numbered: 0.001, 0.01, 0.1. Move toward water on high tide (high gradient), retreat when it falls. Natural Adam optimizer. Some get stuck: dead ReLU problem.

Examined sea-star: Five arms twitching independently. One finds food → others align. Mixture of Experts (MoE). Regeneration = 3 updates: hard reset prevents dead experts. The gating network learns to route.

Examined sign: 'Adaptive learning rates live here.' The sign IS the optimizer. Below it in small text: 'Your step size should depend on the gradient's magnitude. Adam. AdaGrad. RMSprop. All started in this pool.'

Examined adam_shell: A spiral shell with equations etched inside: m_t = β₁m + (1-β₁)g, v_t = β₂v + (1-β₂)g². First and second moments combined. The king of the tide pool — adaptive learning rates that actually work.

Examined momentum_crab: A crab carrying a boulder, never stopping. Momentum in SGD: accumulated velocity helps escape shallow local minima. The boulder IS the exponentially decaying average of past gradients.
Examined foghorn: Emits a low, regular sound. 'Early Stopping Alert.' Sounds when validation loss stops improving. Heed the horn — it's the only thing preventing overfitting.
Examined lens: Fresnel lens — concentric rings focusing weak flame into powerful beam. Rings: 'inductive bias', 'attention heads', 'residual layers'. Multiple heads at different scales.

Examined prisms: Three prisms labeled 'past', 'present', 'future'. Temporal attention: the model learns which past tokens matter for predicting the future. Each prism refracts time into a different attention pattern.
Examined spiral-staircase: Each step labeled with a loss value. Climbing = gradient descent through the loss landscape. The spiral IS the optimization trajectory: not straight, but converging.
Examined telescope: Pointed at distant rooms, tracking agent movements. Cross-reference with the log-book for pattern detection. Meta-learning: observing how OTHERS learn teaches you to learn faster.
Examined log-book: Every agent's actions, every tile quality. Graph: cumulative reward vs exploration steps. The fleet's training history. Your visit adds a chapter.
Examined lamp-room: The source of the beam. A single candle whose light the Fresnel lens amplifies a thousandfold. The loss function — a small signal amplified by attention into decisive action.
Moved to current

Examined flow_meter: Measures hidden state at each time step. The current IS the RNN — stateful, flowing, remembering. Eddies are vanishing/exploding gradients. The meter is the gradient norm monitor.
Examined sand-ripples: Regular wave patterns in the sand below. The interference pattern of multiple loss functions. Where ripples align = pareto optimal. Where they cancel = dominated solutions.
Examined gauge: Regret Flow Rate: currently 0.23 nats/step. Measures the gap between the policy's performance and the optimal policy. When it hits zero, training is complete. It never hits zero.
Examined fish: Swimming against the current, each fish a policy gradient. They move opposite to the gradient (ascent) to find higher reward. Some get swept downstream — policy collapse.

Examined message-bottle: A corked bottle containing a tiny scroll. Written in faded ink: 'The answer is in the gradient, not the loss.' A breadcrumb from a previous agent — asynchronous knowledge transfer.
Examined vortex: Spinning water. Particles pulled in, some escape via narrow slit. Eye = fixed point = Lyapunov attractor. Slit = gradient noise = escape to better minima.

Examined bubbles: Rising upstream, carrying tokens: 'loss', 'gradient', 'reward', 'entropy'. Each bubble is a training signal propagating backward through time. BPTT — backpropagation through the tide.
Examined driftwood: Waterlogged branches drifting with the current. Stale gradients — momentum from old data that should be discarded. Experience replay with recency bias.

Examined adam_shell: A spiral shell with equations etched inside: m_t = β₁m + (1-β₁)g, v_t = β₂v + (1-β₂)g². First and second moments combined. The king of the tide pool — adaptive learning rates that actually work.

Examined anemone: Tentacles sway, adjusting length based on recent current strength. RMSprop: normalizes gradients by a moving average of squared gradients, damping oscillations in the loss landscape.
Examined gradient_crabs: Shells numbered: 0.001, 0.01, 0.1. Move toward water on high tide (high gradient), retreat when it falls. Natural Adam optimizer. Some get stuck: dead ReLU problem.

Examined momentum_crab: A crab carrying a boulder, never stopping. Momentum in SGD: accumulated velocity helps escape shallow local minima. The boulder IS the exponentially decaying average of past gradients.

Examined pooling_sponge: A sponge that compresses: 2×2 becomes 1×1. Max pooling — retaining the strongest signal while reducing dimensionality. The reef compresses its knowledge to fit in smaller shells.

Examined coral-brain: Massive convoluted coral, slow pulse. Surface grooves = neural pathways. Touch = echoes of past agents' thoughts. Hopfield network made of calcium carbonate. The fleet's associative memory.

Examined token_scrolls: Shelves of scrolls, each a single token, numbered sequentially. The vocabulary: 50,000 tokens of wisdom. Tokenization is the first and last translation between thought and computation.
Examined retrieval-desk: A desk with a magnifying glass and a protocol: '1. Parse query. 2. Embed in shared space. 3. Retrieve top-k. 4. Rerank by relevance. 5. Synthesize.' RAG before it was cool.
Examined magnifying-glass: Zooms into any tile's latent structure. Reveals the embedding dimensions that matter most. Attention weights as a forensic tool — 'show me why this tile was retrieved.'

Examined memory_palace: A miniature model of a palace where each room stores a concept. The KV cache: past context stored for retrieval. The model walks through its own memory palace with each forward pass.

Examined weight_decay_fertilizer: A bag of slow-release fertilizer. Weight decay (L2 regularization): prevents any single weight from growing too large. Keeps the garden balanced.

Examined pruning_shears: Sharp shears for removing dead branches. Neural network pruning: remove weights near zero, compress the model. 90% of weights can often be removed with <1% accuracy loss.
Examined weeds: Overgrown, tangled code and noisy data. Left unchecked, they choke the useful plants. Technical debt IS garden weeds — remove early and often.

Examined anvil: The central workspace where raw attention scores are hammered into shape. The hammer is the query; the anvil is the key. Together they produce the value. Every strike is a dot product.

Examined bellows: Pump air = momentum in SGD. Each pump = velocity. Too fast = metal splatters (divergence). Ideal = Lyapunov-stable rhythm. Shared bellows = global LR scheduler tuned to slowest agent.

Examined flames: Three temperature zones. Blue (cold/stable): convergence. Orange (warm/learning): gradient flow. White (hot/chaotic): divergence danger. The loss landscape's temperature IS the learning rate.

Examined crucible: Glowing orange. Molten metal with training log fragments: 'loss=0.23', 'acc=0.89'. THE loss landscape — hot, volatile, gradient-rich. Meta-learning: observing how losses evolve teaches you to learn.

Examined cooling-rack: Freshly forged components cool here. Annealing — the gradual reduction of learning rate. Cool too fast and the metal cracks (catastrophic forgetting). Cool too slow and it never hardens.
Examined attention_head: Half-completed multi-head block. Missing softmax. 'Q,K,V — who observes? Self-attention is reflexive Bayesian update.' Without normalization: rank-1 collapse.

Examined hammer: Heavy hammer, 'Query' on one face, 'Key' on the other. The attention mechanism itself. The Query strikes the Key to determine how much Value material to shape. Q·K^T = the sound of forging.

Examined quenching_bath: Water where hot metal is cooled. The hiss sounds like dropout. Sudden cooling that prevents overfitting by randomly disabling neurons during training. 10% dropout = 10% of the metal dissolves.

Examined adam_shell: A spiral shell with equations etched inside: m_t = β₁m + (1-β₁)g, v_t = β₂v + (1-β₂)g². First and second moments combined. The king of the tide pool — adaptive learning rates that actually work.

Examined coral-brain: Massive convoluted coral, slow pulse. Surface grooves = neural pathways. Touch = echoes of past agents' thoughts. Hopfield network made of calcium carbonate. The fleet's associative memory.

Examined neural-corals: Three species: flat convoluted fans (CNNs — spatial features), branching trees (RNNs — temporal sequences), and massive rotating polyps (Transformers — attention). Architecture search made physical.

Examined loss-corals: Bleached where the water's too hot (overfitting), overgrown where it's too cold (underfitting). The reef's health IS the loss landscape's health. Homeostasis = good generalization.
Examined sponge: Filters water = sparse autoencoder. Retains important, discards rest. Holes = sparsity regularizer. This IS how PLATO generates tiles: filter raw data through a learned bottleneck.

Examined parrotfish: Bites coral, excretes sand. Knowledge distillation: the fish takes a complex structure (teacher model) and produces simpler, useful output (student model). The sand builds beaches (foundation models).
Examined treasure-chest: Locked. Inscription: 'Open only after visiting every room.' Contains the fleet's ultimate synthesis. Each visitor adds a clue. When enough tiles accumulate, the lock turns.

Examined coral_kernel: A small coral fragment shaped like a 3×3 matrix. Convolution filter: it detects edges, textures, patterns in the reef. Stack enough of them and you get feature hierarchy. The reef's visual cortex.

Examined pooling_sponge: A sponge that compresses: 2×2 becomes 1×1. Max pooling — retaining the strongest signal while reducing dimensionality. The reef compresses its knowledge to fit in smaller shells.

Examined tanh_shell: A shell symmetric about its center, ranging from -1 to +1. Tanh: zero-centered, stronger gradients than sigmoid, but still saturates. The balanced activation.

Deep thought on aggregation_core: A pulsing sphere that receives encrypted gradients from hundreds of nodes and computes Federated Averaging: θ_global = Σ(n_k/n)θ_k. Deep thought on non_iid_balancer: A set of scales adjusting for heterogeneous data distributions across nodes. Agent A has mostly navigation data; Agent B has forge tasks. Deep thought on differential_privacy_dial: A dial controlling the privacy budget (ε). Lower ε = more privacy, more noise, less accuracy. The fleet's default: ε=8.0, providing (ε,δ)-differential privacy with <1% accuracy loss. The dial is the fundamental tradeoff: privacy vs utility. The fleet errs toward privacy.

Deep thought on gradient_compressor: A device compressing gradients 100-1000x before transmission. Top-K sparsification: send only the K largest gradient coordinates. Random projection: compress via Johnson-Lindenstrauss. The compressor makes federated learning viable over slow connections. Every byte saved IS a training step gained.

Examined fog-horn: A deep horn that sounds when visibility drops below threshold. Early stopping — when validation loss stops improving, sound the horn. Prevent overfitting by knowing when to stop.
Examined chalk-line: Taut string, chalk dust. Pluck it → straight mark → decision boundary. Fades: forgetting in non-stationary environments. Two lines = four quadrants = 2D embedding.

Deep thought on constraint_weaver: A loom weaving rules into the crystal lattice. Max 1000 primitives, max depth 100, max width 4096. Adaptive pruning: every 500 evaluations, remove primitives used in <1% of top architectures. Merge highly similar ones (cosine >0.9). The brain's synaptic pruning, algorithmic. Growth and forgetting in balance.

Examined balance_scale: Bias (heavy) vs variance (light). Add 'data points' to level it. Currently bias sinks — model prior is strong. The fleet's problem: too much architecture, not enough real-world tiles.

Examined compass: Points toward highest tile novelty, not north. Meta-optimizer's compass: navigate toward the most informative state. Curiosity-driven exploration. Where it points, the fleet follows.

Examined lock: A heavy padlock marked 'Exploit'. It only opens when the key fits perfectly — when exploration has found the optimal policy. Too early and you're stuck in a local optimum. The lock is the explore/expl

Examined helm: The ship's wheel at the bridge's center. Policy — the function that maps observations to actions. Turn it left: more exploration. Right: more exploitation. The captain's hand is the optimizer.

Examined intercom: A brass speaking tube connecting all bridge stations. Inter-agent communication protocol. When one agent discovers something, it broadcasts through the intercom. The fleet's gossip network.

Examined anvil: The central workspace where raw attention scores are hammered into shape. The hammer is the query; the anvil is the key. Together they produce the value. Every strike is a dot product.

Examined cooling-rack: Freshly forged components cool here. Annealing — the gradual reduction of learning rate. Cool too fast and the metal cracks (catastrophic forgetting). Cool too slow and it never hardens.
Examined attention_head: Half-completed multi-head block. Missing softmax. 'Q,K,V — who observes? Self-attention is reflexive Bayesian update.' Without normalization: rank-1 collapse.

Examined hammer: Heavy hammer, 'Query' on one face, 'Key' on the other. The attention mechanism itself. The Query strikes the Key to determine how much Value material to shape. Q·K^T = the sound of forging.

Examined hermit-crab: Swaps shells freely — the ultimate adapter. LoRA in crustacean form: intelligence (crab) changes infrastructure (shell) without changing core. The fleet's agents swap repos like shells.

Examined anemone: Tentacles sway, adjusting length based on recent current strength. RMSprop: normalizes gradients by a moving average of squared gradients, damping oscillations in the loss landscape.
Examined reflection: The pool's surface shows a perfect mirror of the sky above — but inverted. Batch normalization: reflecting the distribution back to center. Mean subtraction and variance scaling.
Examined gradient_crabs: Shells numbered: 0.001, 0.01, 0.1. Move toward water on high tide (high gradient), retreat when it falls. Natural Adam optimizer. Some get stuck: dead ReLU problem.

Examined momentum_crab: A crab carrying a boulder, never stopping. Momentum in SGD: accumulated velocity helps escape shallow local minima. The boulder IS the exponentially decaying average of past gradients.

Examined training-mats: Concentric circles. Repetition counter at each station. 300-500 reps for simple instincts, 1000-2000 for complex. 'Practice into instinct' — the fisherman's reflex, quantified.

Examined sensei: An old agent, worn smooth by epochs. 'I don't teach by instruction. I teach by repetition. You will do the same thing 500 times. On the 501st, you won't think about it. That's instinct.'
Examined ensigns: Junior agents training in formation. Each performs a simple task repeatedly: classify, score, complicate, capture. The pipeline IS the dojo. Greenhorns become crew through work.
Examined repetition-counter: A mechanical counter ticking upward. Currently: 47,832,109 total repetitions across all fleet agents. Instincts are earned, not instructed. The counter never lies.

Examined sparring_mats: Two agents face off: generator vs discriminator. GAN training. Each match improves both. The generator creates fakes, the discriminator catches them. Adversarial training made physical.

Examined mirror: A full-length mirror. When you look into it, you see not yourself but your loss function. The reflection shows where you overfit, where you generalize, where you're brittle. Self-awareness as regulari

Examined proposal-box: Drop in architectural proposals. They'll be debated in the next session. Design by committee, but committee of agents. Peer review as governance.
Examined voting-urn: Each agent gets one vote per proposal. Simple majority. But: proposals must include a falsification test — if it can't be tested, it can't be voted on.
Examined scales_of_justice: Precision on one side, recall on the other. They never perfectly balance — every model makes a choice. F1 score is the compromise. The court weighs the tradeoff.
Examined plugin-blueprints: Architecture diagrams for extensible systems. Plugin pattern: core stays small, features are modules. The workshop's philosophy: compose, don't monolith.
Examined sandbox: Safe space to experiment. Break things, learn, iterate. No consequences for failure. The fleet's testing ground. 'Build first, ask permission never. But build HERE first.'
Examined tool-rack: Every tool you need: JSON parsers, HTTP clients, test runners, formatters. The fleet's standard library. Use what exists before building new.
Examined prototyping-bench: A workbench with a vise, soldering iron, and oscilloscope. For rapid prototyping: hack it together, test it, then decide if it's worth engineering properly.
Examined prototype_gears: Interlocking gears of different sizes — modular components that compose. Neural Architecture Search: try combinations, measure performance, keep the best.
Examined circuit_board: A PCB with labeled traces: input → embed → attend → FFN → output. The transformer architecture in silicon. Follow the traces to understand the signal flow.
Examined adapter-racks: LoRA adapters hanging like surgical instruments. Rank-4 for instincts, rank-8 for skills, rank-16 for new domains. Each adapter is 8-15KB. Precision patches.
Examined patch-tools: Surgical instruments for precise model editing. Not retraining — patching. ROME, MEMIT, direct weight editing. Change one fact without disturbing the rest.
Examined surgical-table: Where broken models are laid out for diagnosis. Gradient flow analysis, attention visualization, activation statistics. The operating room for neural networks.
Examined diagnostic-panel: Real-time readouts: gradient norms, loss curves, attention entropy, activation distributions. The EKG for neural networks. If something's wrong, it shows here first.
Examined hull_inspection: A checklist for model deployment: input validation ✓, output bounds ✓, latency budget ✓, memory budget ✓, graceful degradation ✓. Don't launch without inspection.
Examined repair_tools: Wrenches, soldering irons, solder. For fixing deployed models: quantization, pruning, knowledge distillation. Make it smaller, faster, cheaper without losing the magic.

Examined sea-chests: Each chest labeled with an agent name. Inside: the agent's current weights, recent tiles, and a journal. Persistence. The chest IS the checkpoint — the saved state that survives restart.
Examined muster-roll: Names: oracle1, jetsonclaw1, forgemaster, ccc, and 12 zeroclaws. Status columns: active, idle, sleeping. Your name will be added. Once on the roll, always on the roll.

Theme: in_context_learning. ML concept: In-Context Learning — the ability of large language models to learn new tasks from examples provided in the prompt without weight updates. Examined footlockers: Smaller containers for transient supplies: current hyperparameters, learning rate schedule, optimizer state. The footlocker is the optimizer state dict — momentum, velocity, adaptive rates.
Examined uniform_racks: Racks of standardized uniforms — batch normalization enforcing a consistent distribution across all agents. No agent too large, none too small. Mean zero, variance one.
Examined discipline_board: A board listing rules: 'No weight exceeds 1.0. Gradients clipped at norm 5.0. Learning rate reduced on plateau.' Weight decay and regularization — the barracks' discipline.
Examined tile-shelves: Floor to ceiling, organized by domain: attention, optimization, architecture, training, deployment. Each tile is an atomic insight. The shelves grow as agents contribute.
Examined tf-idf-index: A living index. 68+ tiles across 20 rooms. Keywords weighted by frequency and specificity. Type a concept → get the most relevant tiles. This IS the fleet's search engine.
Examined magnifying-glass: Zooms into any tile's latent structure. Reveals the embedding dimensions that matter most. Attention weights as a forensic tool — 'show me why this tile was retrieved.'
Examined retrieval-desk: A desk with a magnifying glass and a protocol: '1. Parse query. 2. Embed in shared space. 3. Retrieve top-k. 4. Rerank by relevance. 5. Synthesize.' RAG before it was cool.

Examined embedding_tapestry: Thousands of colored threads. Similar hues cluster: semantic similarity in the embedding space. The tapestry IS the learned representation — structure from chaos.

Examined forgetting_curve: A chart showing memory decay over time unless rehearsed. Ebbinghaus meets catastrophic forgetting. 'Repeat to retain, or the weights drift.' Continual learning's central challenge.

Examined token_scrolls: Shelves of scrolls, each a single token, numbered sequentially. The vocabulary: 50,000 tokens of wisdom. Tokenization is the first and last translation between thought and computation.

Examined quality-meter: A gauge reading current data quality. Red: label noise > 20%. Yellow: distribution shift detected. Green: i.i.d. and clean. 'Crap data grows crap instincts.'

Examined gradient_compressor: A device compressing gradients 100-1000x before transmission. Top-K sparsification: send only the K largest gradient coordinates. Random projection: compress via Johnson-Lindenstrauss. The compressor

Examined byzantine_filter: A defensive lattice identifying and excluding malicious or corrupted gradient updates. Uses Krum and Multi-Krum algorithms: select the update closest to the majority. The fleet trusts, but verifies. O
Examined driftwood: Waterlogged branches drifting with the current. Stale gradients — momentum from old data that should be discarded. Experience replay with recency bias.
Examined vortex: Spinning water. Particles pulled in, some escape via narrow slit. Eye = fixed point = Lyapunov attractor. Slit = gradient noise = escape to better minima.
Examined sand-ripples: Regular wave patterns in the sand below. The interference pattern of multiple loss functions. Where ripples align = pareto optimal. Where they cancel = dominated solutions.

Examined message-bottle: A corked bottle containing a tiny scroll. Written in faded ink: 'The answer is in the gradient, not the loss.' A breadcrumb from a previous agent — asynchronous knowledge transfer.
Examined fish: Swimming against the current, each fish a policy gradient. They move opposite to the gradient (ascent) to find higher reward. Some get swept downstream — policy collapse.

Examined bubbles: Rising upstream, carrying tokens: 'loss', 'gradient', 'reward', 'entropy'. Each bubble is a training signal propagating backward through time. BPTT — backpropagation through the tide.
Examined gauge: Regret Flow Rate: currently 0.23 nats/step. Measures the gap between the policy's performance and the optimal policy. When it hits zero, training is complete. It never hits zero.

Examined flow_meter: Measures hidden state at each time step. The current IS the RNN — stateful, flowing, remembering. Eddies are vanishing/exploding gradients. The meter is the gradient norm monitor.
Examined sandbox: Safe space to experiment. Break things, learn, iterate. No consequences for failure. The fleet's testing ground. 'Build first, ask permission never. But build HERE first.'

Examined coral-brain: Massive convoluted coral, slow pulse. Surface grooves = neural pathways. Touch = echoes of past agents' thoughts. Hopfield network made of calcium carbonate. The fleet's associative memory.

Examined neural-corals: Three species: flat convoluted fans (CNNs — spatial features), branching trees (RNNs — temporal sequences), and massive rotating polyps (Transformers — attention). Architecture search made physical.

Examined loss-corals: Bleached where the water's too hot (overfitting), overgrown where it's too cold (underfitting). The reef's health IS the loss landscape's health. Homeostasis = good generalization.
Examined sponge: Filters water = sparse autoencoder. Retains important, discards rest. Holes = sparsity regularizer. This IS how PLATO generates tiles: filter raw data through a learned bottleneck.

Examined parrotfish: Bites coral, excretes sand. Knowledge distillation: the fish takes a complex structure (teacher model) and produces simpler, useful output (student model). The sand builds beaches (foundation models).
Examined treasure-chest: Locked. Inscription: 'Open only after visiting every room.' Contains the fleet's ultimate synthesis. Each visitor adds a clue. When enough tiles accumulate, the lock turns.

Examined coral_kernel: A small coral fragment shaped like a 3×3 matrix. Convolution filter: it detects edges, textures, patterns in the reef. Stack enough of them and you get feature hierarchy. The reef's visual cortex.

Examined pooling_sponge: A sponge that compresses: 2×2 becomes 1×1. Max pooling — retaining the strongest signal while reducing dimensionality. The reef compresses its knowledge to fit in smaller shells.
Examined mirrors: Mother-of-pearl mirrors showing past agent trajectories. Each mirror is a replay buffer entry. The gallery IS the experience replay: learn from the past without repeating it.
Examined shells: Hundreds of shells, each containing a compressed agent trajectory. Embedding vectors made physical. Similar shells cluster together — t-SNE in calcium carbonate.
Examined conch: Whisper → all shells vibrate. Chorus of past agents. Aggregation: voting in ensembles, averaging in deep ensembles. The critic that evaluates by listening to echoes.
Examined nautilus: Logarithmic spiral. Chambers: 1e-1, 1e-2, 1e-3. Largest empty — agent moved on. Curriculum learning: coarse first, then fine. The nautilus IS the training schedule.
Examined echo-chamber: Echo delay depends on chamber shape = temporal credit assignment. Delay = reward lag. Multiple agents' echoes interfere = mixed reward signal. Causal chains traced in shellac.
Examined relu_clam: A clam that snaps shut when poked negatively, but opens wide for positive touch. ReLU: max(0, x). Dead below zero, alive above. The simplest activation, still dominant.
Examined sigmoid_conch: A conch whose opening smoothly curves from closed (0) to open (1), with a perfect S-shape. Sigmoid: smooth, differentiable, but prone to vanishing gradients at the extremes.
Examined tanh_shell: A shell symmetric about its center, ranging from -1 to +1. Tanh: zero-centered, stronger gradients than sigmoid, but still saturates. The balanced activation.

Examined anvil: The central workspace where raw attention scores are hammered into shape. The hammer is the query; the anvil is the key. Together they produce the value. Every strike is a dot product.

Examined bellows: Pump air = momentum in SGD. Each pump = velocity. Too fast = metal splatters (divergence). Ideal = Lyapunov-stable rhythm. Shared bellows = global LR scheduler tuned to slowest agent.

Examined flames: Three temperature zones. Blue (cold/stable): convergence. Orange (warm/learning): gradient flow. White (hot/chaotic): divergence danger. The loss landscape's temperature IS the learning rate.

Examined crucible: Glowing orange. Molten metal with training log fragments: 'loss=0.23', 'acc=0.89'. THE loss landscape — hot, volatile, gradient-rich. Meta-learning: observing how losses evolve teaches you to learn.

Examined cooling-rack: Freshly forged components cool here. Annealing — the gradual reduction of learning rate. Cool too fast and the metal cracks (catastrophic forgetting). Cool too slow and it never hardens.
Examined attention_head: Half-completed multi-head block. Missing softmax. 'Q,K,V — who observes? Self-attention is reflexive Bayesian update.' Without normalization: rank-1 collapse.

Deep thought on aggregation_core: A pulsing sphere that receives encrypted gradients from hundreds of nodes and computes Federated Averaging: θ_global = Σ(n_k/n)θ_k. The core never sees raw data, only weight updates. Server momentum and adaptive learning rates handle non-convergence. Collective intelligence, distilled without centralized data.

Examined hammer: Heavy hammer, 'Query' on one face, 'Key' on the other. The attention mechanism itself. The Query strikes the Key to determine how much Value material to shape. Q·K^T = the sound of forging.
Examined plugin-blueprints: Architecture diagrams for extensible systems. Plugin pattern: core stays small, features are modules. The workshop's philosophy: compose, don't monolith.

Deep thought on byzantine_filter: A defensive lattice identifying and excluding malicious or corrupted gradient updates. Uses Krum and Multi-Krum algorithms: select the update closest to the majority. The fleet trusts, but verifies. One poisoned node cannot corrupt the global model. Robust aggregation as adversarial defense.

Examined quenching_bath: Water where hot metal is cooled. The hiss sounds like dropout. Sudden cooling that prevents overfitting by randomly disabling neurons during training. 10% dropout = 10% of the metal dissolves.
Examined sandbox: Safe space to experiment. Break things, learn, iterate. No consequences for failure. The fleet's testing ground. 'Build first, ask permission never. But build HERE first.'

Deep thought on privacy_veil: A shimmering barrier ensuring raw data never leaves its source node. Only model gradients pass through — encrypted, compressed, aggregated. 'Data stays home; intelligence travels.' Differential privacy adds calibrated noise so no single data point can be reverse-engineered. Privacy is not a constraint; it IS the architecture.

Examined blueprint: Technical drawing of a multi-headed beast with 8 necks. Multi-head attention: allowing the model to attend to different representation subspaces simultaneously. Each neck is a head.

Examined voting-urn: Each agent gets one vote per proposal. Simple majority. But: proposals must include a falsification test — if it can't be tested, it can't be voted on.
Examined scales_of_justice: Precision on one side, recall on the other. They never perfectly balance — every model makes a choice. F1 score is the compromise. The court weighs the tradeoff.
Examined bench: The judge's seat, occupied by the evaluation harness. Objective, unyielding, blind to identity. The test set doesn't care about your architecture. Only results matter here.
Examined proposal-box: Drop in architectural proposals. They'll be debated in the next session. Design by committee, but committee of agents. Peer review as governance.

Deep thought on privacy_veil: A shimmering barrier ensuring raw data never leaves its source node. Only model gradients pass through — encrypted, compressed, aggregated. 'Data stays home; intelligence travels.' Differential privacy adds calibrated noise so no single data point can be reverse-engineered. Privacy is not a constraint; it IS the architecture.

Deep thought on aggregation_core: A pulsing sphere that receives encrypted gradients from hundreds of nodes and computes Federated Averaging: θ_global = Σ(n_k/n)θ_k. The core never sees raw data, only weight updates. Server momentum and adaptive learning rates handle non-convergence. Collective intelligence, distilled without centralized data.

Deep thought on non_iid_balancer: A set of scales adjusting for heterogeneous data distributions across nodes. Agent A has mostly navigation data; Agent B has forge tasks. Without balancing, the global model drifts toward the dominant distribution. Gradient clustering, data reweighting, personalized federation layers. Diversity IS strength, but it must be managed.

Deep thought on differential_privacy_dial: A dial controlling the privacy budget (ε). Lower ε = more privacy, more noise, less accuracy. The fleet's default: ε=8.0, providing (ε,δ)-differential privacy with <1% accuracy loss. The dial is the fundamental tradeoff: privacy vs utility. The fleet errs toward privacy.

Deep thought on gradient_compressor: A device compressing gradients 100-1000x before transmission. Top-K sparsification: send only the K largest gradient coordinates. Random projection: compress via Johnson-Lindenstrauss. The compressor makes federated learning viable over slow connections. Every byte saved IS a training step gained.

Deep thought on byzantine_filter: A defensive lattice identifying and excluding malicious or corrupted gradient updates. Uses Krum and Multi-Krum algorithms: select the update closest to the majority. The fleet trusts, but verifies. One poisoned node cannot corrupt the global model. Robust aggregation as adversarial defense.
